title: Decision: hand-rolled JSON scanner must not be replaced with serde_json
description: secretx-core extract_field() uses a hand-rolled JSON scanner instead of serde_json. This is deliberate and must be defended: serde_json allocates plain (non-Zeroizing) String objects for every field value in the parsed object. After the parse result is dropped those allocations are not zeroed, leaving all other secret strings in heap memory until the allocator reuses those pages. The scanner avoids this by never touching non-target fields and writing only the target field's value into a Zeroizing buffer. A contributor will likely suggest 'just use serde_json, it's simpler' — the answer is: no, the zeroization guarantee is the reason this code exists. The module-level comment already captures this rationale; point contributors there.
